codeReview.summary: TTY-isolation primitives were implemented (`exec </dev/null` + `script -q /dev/null`), but there are blocking reliability gaps in process lifecycle handling and exit-status reporting that can produce orphaned mitmdump processes and false-positive harness passes.

issues.description: The mitmdump process is started before entering the fixture's teardown guard, but startup checks (`wait_for_port`, CA-file wait) run before the `try/finally` at line 108. If either startup check raises, the fixture exits without terminating `process`, violating the harness no-orphan expectation and contaminating later runs. Wrap startup + yield in a single `try/finally` (or explicit startup-failure cleanup) so `process.terminate()/kill()` always runs.

On app-exit timeout, the driver treats missing app-exit state as success by default (`{"returncode": 0, "forced_exit": true}`) when `require_app_exit` is unset. This can silently mark hung or undismissed setup subprocesses as passing. The smoke test also leaves `require_app_exit` unset (`client/tests/tuistory/test_harness.py:22`), so `assert result.app_returncode == 0` (`line 57`) can pass even after forced session close rather than true app completion.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `tui-tests-worker` skill assumes the `~/.bun/bin/tuistory` shim is usable, but this environment required a Bun CLI fallback to `~/.bun/install/global/node_modules/tuistory/dist/cli.js`. The worker had to discover and document this ad hoc.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/tui-tests-worker/SKILL.md states tuistory is available at `~/.bun/bin/tuistory`; handoff.discoveredIssues reports broken shim + fallback; `.factory/library/tuistory-harness.md` was added to capture the workaround.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Dry-run procedure wording is ambiguous about whether the intercepted HTTPS proof must originate from the setup subprocess itself versus a wrapper-side probe, which led to a procedural deviation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff.skillFeedback.deviations (step 6) says worker used wrapper-managed probe traffic to satisfy interception evidence and requested clarification in handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges.

summary: Review failed with two blocking issues: mitmdump can leak on fixture startup failure, and the driver can report forced-close sessions as successful. Both issues directly affect harness reliability and can mask real end-to-end failures.
